title: Police investigating after dead body found near home owned by Pressley’s husband
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police found a dead body near a Mattapan property owned by Ayanna Pressley's husband on Saturday.
The property at 25 Malta St., valued over $919,000, is a rental and currently on the market for sale.
Neighbors noted a previous raid two months ago with tenants, including teenagers, living at the property.

title: Healey aims to close great divide of resale ticket prices with new proposal
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey proposes 'The Great Divide Act' to cap ticket resale prices in Massachusetts at 110% of face value.
The act aims to limit reseller fees to 10% and crack down on deceptive ticket selling practices.
Vermont singer-songwriter Noah Kahan supports the legislation, which mirrors Vermont's existing ticket price cap law.

title: Rhode Island couple unexpectedly become parents while on vacation in Florida
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lauren Siwinski and Gary Glenn unexpectedly became parents while vacationing in Tampa Bay, Florida.
Siwinski experienced abdominal pain and learned at the hospital she was pregnant and in labor.
The baby boy, named Sebastian, was delivered safely and met by family members in Florida.

title: Juvenile humpback whale washes ashore in Winthrop
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 35,000-pound juvenile humpback whale was found lodged under a dock in Winthrop, Massachusetts, on Wednesday.
Marine rescue teams, including WDC-NA and NOAA, are investigating the whale's cause of death and planning a necropsy.
The incident marks the first large whale stranding in Massachusetts this year, and an investigation is ongoing.

title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jahmai Jones hit his first home run for the Red Sox in a game against the Rays at Fenway Park.
Jones's two-run homer in the second inning tied the game 2-2 during his second plate appearance for Boston.
Traded from the Tigers, Jones struggled with a .135 average before the move to the Red Sox.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit consecutive multi-home run games for the Red Sox, a feat last achieved by Mookie Betts in 2016.
Abreu's performance helped the Red Sox win against the Tampa Bay Rays, elevating them above a .500 record.
At Fenway Park, Abreu became the second Red Sox player, after George Scott in 1971, to achieve back-to-back multi-HR games.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox extend win streak to 12 games with a comeback victory over the Tampa Bay Rays.
Wilyer Abreu hits two home runs in consecutive games, aiding the Red Sox's win at Fenway Park.
The Red Sox maintain the final wild-card spot in the American League, with a four-game sweep of the Rays imminent.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James reportedly considers Boston Celtics as his least preferred team to join in free agency.
James's decision is delaying the NBA's schedule announcement for the 2026-27 season as he narrows down team options.
The Heat, Cavaliers, and Warriors are rumored as potential destinations for James's final NBA career move.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady participated in a panel at Fanatics Fest in NYC with Logan Paul, leading to a viral face slap.
The incident between Brady and Paul followed prior tension, with Paul teasing Brady over a flag football loss.
Aside from the altercation, Brady showcased his skills, throwing a baseball accurately and participating in other activities.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men charged with kidnapping and assaulting a 28-year-old near Winter Park on Thursday.
Suspects identified as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett, all from Nantucket.
Victim was allegedly beaten, tied with an extension cord, and taken to a hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.
